analysis_result:
Frequency of 6p arm loss in MPNST-G2 tumors (n=8), using a Python methylprep log2-ratio CONUMEE substitute with Benign_NF-G4 (n=20) as the diploid reference:
  - At arm-level threshold log2-ratio <= -0.10:  3/8 = 37.5% (samples M1677, M1933, M2491)
  - At arm-level threshold log2-ratio <= -0.15:  0/8 = 0.0% (most-negative sample M2491 was -0.149)
Interpreting the requested -0.10 to -0.15 window inclusively, point-estimate frequency = 37.5% (3/8). MPNST_G2 6p log2-ratio distribution: median -0.057, range -0.149 to +0.024. Benign_NF-G4 reference 6p log2-ratio: mean 0.001, SD 0.015 (tight around 0 as expected for diploid reference).
denominator_used: N = 8 MPNST_G2 tumors (methylation subgroup) with MethylationEPIC IDAT + usable 6p probe signal (30,744 of 30,888 6p cg probes retained after dropping probes with any missing total intensity in the 28-sample MPNST_G2 + Benign_NF-G4 working set). Reference denominator: N = 20 Benign_NF-G4 tumors.
assumptions_made: Genome build hg19 (EPIC manifest GRCh37 coordinates); chromosome 6 centromere placed at 58,830,166 bp, so 6p = CHR=6 AND MAPINFO<58,830,166 (30,744 valid probes); total per-probe intensity = sum of methylated + unmethylated signal: Type I Grn probes use Grn[AddressA]+Grn[AddressB], Type I Red probes use Red[AddressA]+Red[AddressB], Type II probes use Grn[AddressA]+Red[AddressA]; per-sample normalization = median-centering of log2(total intensity+1) so each sample has the same global median log2 intensity; per-probe log2 ratio = centered log2(sample) - median(centered log2 intensity across the 20 Benign_NF-G4 reference samples); arm-level log2 ratio = median per-probe log2 ratio across all valid 6p cg probes (CONUMEE-style arm summary); loss calls applied at the two thresholds given in the prompt (-0.10 and -0.15), with no requirement that 6q be copy-neutral; only cg-prefixed probes on autosomes + X/Y used (rs and ch probes excluded); beta-based background correction (Noob, dye-bias) and BMIQ not applied because this is a methylprep log2-ratio substitute for CONUMEE rather than the full conumee R pipeline.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - Clinical: data/41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx (108 samples x 15 cols) -- fields used: Tumor_ID, Sentrix_ID, Methylation_Group.
   - EPIC manifest: data/GSE207207_idats/GPL21145_MethylationEPIC_15073387_v-1-0.csv.gz (864,935 cg probes after skiprows=7 and filter to cg-prefix); fields used: IlmnID, AddressA_ID, AddressB_ID, Infinium_Design_Type, Color_Channel, CHR, MAPINFO.
   - IDAT files: data/GSE207207_idats/*.idat.gz (217 gzipped IDATs covering all 108 samples, Grn+Red per sample).

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Index: Methylation_Group in {MPNST_G2, Benign_NF-G4}.
   - MPNST_G2 (analytic group): 8 tumors (M1677, M1933, M2325, M2377, M2491, M4719, M9413, M9450).
   - Benign_NF-G4 (CNV reference): 20 tumors.
   - Working set for intensity matrix: 28 samples. Probes retained: cg-probes on chr1-22,X,Y with non-missing total intensity in all 28 samples -> 862,927 rows.

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Per-probe total intensity T = M + U, computed directly from raw IDATs using methylprep IdatDataset:
       Type I Grn: T = Grn[AddressA] + Grn[AddressB]
       Type I Red: T = Red[AddressA] + Red[AddressB]
       Type II:    T = Grn[AddressA] + Red[AddressA]
   - Per-sample normalization: log2(T+1), then center each sample by subtracting its global median log2 intensity (so each sample has median log2 intensity = 0 across all autosomal cg probes, controlling for plate/array-level total signal differences).
   - Per-probe log2 ratio R_ij = centered_log2_T_ij - median_over_NF4( centered_log2_T_ij ), i.e. sample minus the Benign_NF-G4 reference median. This is the Python methylprep equivalent of a CONUMEE sample-vs-control-panel log2 ratio.
   - 6p definition: CHR='6' AND MAPINFO < 58,830,166 (hg19 chr6 centromere). 30,888 manifest-listed 6p probes; 30,744 retained after the non-missing-intensity filter.
   - Arm-level log2 ratio per sample = median(R_ij) across all 6p probes.
   - Loss call: arm_log2r <= threshold. Thresholds evaluated: -0.10 and -0.15.

D. COMPUTATION
   Arm-level 6p log2-ratio per sample (sorted):
     Benign_NF-G4 (n=20): mean = 0.001, SD = 0.015, median = -0.0015, full range -0.017 to +0.053. No reference tumor approaches the -0.10 threshold -- good calibration of the pipeline.
     MPNST_G2 (n=8):
         M2491  -0.149   LOSS at -0.10 (borderline at -0.15)
         M1933  -0.130   LOSS at -0.10
         M1677  -0.114   LOSS at -0.10
         M4719  -0.094
         M2377  -0.024
         M9413  -0.021
         M2325  -0.002
         M9450  +0.024
   Loss frequency in MPNST_G2:
     threshold -0.10:  3/8 = 37.5%
     threshold -0.15:  0/8 = 0.0% (M2491 at -0.149 is the only sample within 0.01 of -0.15)
   Since 6q did not show systematic parallel loss (MPNST_G2 6q log2-ratios ranged -0.195 to +0.009), the 6p depressions are arm-selective rather than whole-chromosome-6 effects.

E. RESULT
   6p loss frequency in MPNST-G2 (CONUMEE log2-ratio substitute, Benign_NF-G4 reference):
     - At threshold -0.10:  3/8 (37.5%)
     - At threshold -0.15:  0/8 (0.0%)
     Point estimate reported: 37.5% (3/8), with the caveat that one additional sample (M2491, arm-median log2r = -0.149) sits on the border of the stricter threshold.

F. CAVEATS
   - Not the full CONUMEE R pipeline: no Noob background correction, no bin-based local segmentation, no IQR-based per-probe control-panel weighting. The log2-ratio arm-median substitute is a reasonable first-pass but likely gives slightly more muted arm-level magnitudes than CONUMEE's detail-bin-weighted segmentation.
   - Normal-sample contamination / low tumor purity in any MPNST_G2 tumor would dilute log2-ratio magnitude and could push a true single-copy 6p loss below (closer to zero than) the -0.15 threshold.
   - Small N (8) -- 37.5% has a wide binomial 95% CI of roughly 9-76%.
   - Sex mismatch between tumor and reference could skew chrX log2-ratios but does not affect chr6p.
   - Threshold interpretation: the prompt specified a -0.10 to -0.15 window; at the permissive end (-0.10) 3/8 samples are called, at the stringent end (-0.15) 0/8 are called. Reported frequency uses the permissive threshold since log2 arm-medians are typically attenuated by tumor impurity and by the median-aggregation step.
